content:
Suno Ai

Ai Music

Passive Income

Youtube Strategy 2023

Artificial Intelligence

10 min read
Just now

--

25 tested prompts across the five audio categories your competitors haven’t discovered yet.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Here’s something the Suno AI community hasn’t fully figured out yet.
Lo-fi hip-hop is saturated. Rain sounds are saturated. “Study music playlist” is saturated.
But five audio niches are sitting wide open right now — pulling millions of YouTube views, high AdSense CPMs, and dedicated audiences who listen for hours at a time — and almost nobody is building in them with Suno.
I spent the last several weeks testing prompts specifically in these five categories. What follows is 25 of the ones that consistently produce professional-quality output — the kind you can upload, monetize, and build a faceless channel around.
Before the prompts: five rules that apply to every single one of them.

5 Rules That Make Every Prompt Work Better
Rule 1 — Lead with specificity, not genre. “Ambient music” tells Suno almost nothing. “Warm candlelit jazz trio in a small club at midnight” tells it everything it needs.
Rule 2 — Name what’s absent. For atmospheric and instrumental content, what you exclude defines the track as much as what you include. Always specify: no vocals, no sudden dynamic changes, no percussion (when relevant).
Rule 3 — Describe texture with material words. Words like “dusty,” “glassy,” “warm,” “cold,” “heavy,” “airy” give Suno a physical sensation to build toward. These produce more consistent results than musical descriptors alone.
Rule 4 — Include pacing language, not just BPM. “Hypnotic and slow” or “unhurried and ancient” works alongside BPM to shape how the track breathes. Use both.
Rule 5 — Add a loop instruction for long-form content. If you’re building 1-hour or 10-hour videos, include “minimal variation” or “designed to loop seamlessly” — this keeps the track from resolving in a way that sounds finished.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Niche 1 — Dark Academia: The Seasonal Traffic Machine
Dark academia dominates search during autumn and winter. The audience is readers, students, writers, and fans of gothic literature. Watch time per session is extremely high — people leave these tracks running for full study sessions.
The CPM in this category is strong because the audience skews toward books, courses, and premium products.

Prompt 1 — The Victorian Library “Instrumental only, no vocals. Dark academia ambient. Heavy rain against tall library windows, distant thunder rolling slowly. Solo cello playing a slow, melancholic phrase that repeats with slight variation. Warm candlelight texture in the low frequencies. The feeling of reading alone in a very old building at 11pm. 65 BPM, D minor. Minimal variation, designed to loop. Deep reverb on the cello, slight room sound.”

Prompt 2 — Gothic Stone Corridors “Instrumental only, no vocals. Gothic atmospheric ambient. Cold stone reverb, the sound of a very old building breathing. Low organ drone holding a single chord. Distant wind through high windows. No melody — pure texture and atmosphere. The feeling of walking through a medieval university at night. 50 BPM. Sustained. Minimal variation.”

Prompt 3–1920s Study Room (Muffled) “Instrumental only, no vocals. Vintage jazz filtered to sound like it’s coming through a wall — slightly muffled, warm, distant. Brushed snare, quiet upright bass, soft piano. The feeling of being in the room next door to a small jazz performance. Nostalgic and focused. 80 BPM. Background texture, not foreground music.”

Prompt 4 — Baroque Clockwork Study “Instrumental only, no vocals. Neoclassical instrumental. Precise harpsichord playing an intricate but repetitive baroque phrase. Subtle rhythmic ticking underneath — not a drum, a mechanism. The feeling of a clockmaker’s workshop in 1740. Focused and elegant. 88 BPM, A minor. Clean acoustic recording texture.”

Prompt 5 — Candlelit Writing Desk “Instrumental only, no vocals. Pure texture. The soft scratch of a quill on paper as a texture element. Distant thunder. Warm cello holding long sustained notes. Solo piano entering occasionally with slow, unresolved chords. The feeling of writing something important by candlelight. 68 BPM. Intimate and solitary.”

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Niche 2 — Deep Space and Astral: The Long-Session Sleep Market
While everyone is uploading rain and ocean sounds, the space ambient and astral projection niche is quietly accumulating tens of millions of watch hours annually.
The audience sleeps with headphones and earbuds — session lengths average 6–8 hours. That is an extraordinary watch time number that drives algorithm performance over time.

Prompt 6 — Zero Gravity Drift “Instrumental only, no vocals. Deep space ambient. Complete weightlessness. Slow evolving synth pads that shift almost imperceptibly over time. Sub-bass rumble suggesting scale and distance. No melody — pure spatial atmosphere. The feeling of floating in deep space with no reference point. 40 BPM. Sustained. Designed to loop.”

Prompt 7 — Interstellar Passage “Instrumental only, no vocals. Cinematic space ambient. A sense of vast distance being covered slowly. Shimmering high-frequency textures like starlight. Low foundation of dark, slow-moving synth. Awe and smallness. Unhurried pace, hypnagogic feeling. Minimal variation, no resolution.”

Prompt 8 — Red Planet Isolation “Instrumental only, no vocals. Sci-fi soundscape, pure texture. Desolate wind across a barren surface. Distant metallic structural hum. No music — only environmental sound design. The feeling of being the only living thing on a planet. Cold and still. No BPM. Sustained atmospheric texture.”

Prompt 9 — Nebula Meditation “Instrumental only, no vocals. Ambient meditation. Slow ethereal synth pads in major key, warm and expansive. Sub-bass drone providing grounding. The feeling of floating through a nebula — colorful, vast, peaceful. 50 BPM. No percussion. Minimal variation. Designed for sleep and deep relaxation.”

Prompt 10 — Neon City Rain (Distant) “Instrumental only, no vocals. Dark ambient, urban dystopia. Distant city sounds filtered to texture — not foreground. Slow dark synth holding long notes. Rain on glass. The feeling of watching a city from a high window at 3am. Peaceful despite the darkness. 55 BPM, D minor. No percussion.”

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Niche 3 — Luxury Lounge and Bossa Nova: The B2B Revenue Source
This is one of the most underestimated niches for sustainable income.
Coffee shops, boutique hotels, coworking spaces, and restaurants stream this content on their displays 24 hours a day. This is genuine B2B watch time — which is among the highest CPM categories on YouTube. A single video placed in the right context can accumulate passive view hours indefinitely.

Prompt 11 — Five-Star Hotel Lobby “Instrumental only, no vocals. Luxury lounge music. Elegant solo grand piano with very light touch. Soft upright bass walking slowly underneath. Distant ambient sound of a high-ceiling interior space. Sophisticated and unhurried. The feeling of arriving somewhere expensive. 80 BPM. Smooth and continuous.”

Prompt 12 — Coastal Bossa Nova at Sunset “Instrumental only, no vocals. Acoustic bossa nova. Nylon string guitar playing gentle syncopated patterns. Very light brushed percussion. Warm bass. The feeling of sitting on a terrace above the ocean as the sun goes down. Relaxed and slightly melancholic in a content way. 92 BPM, G major.”

Prompt 13 — Late Night Jazz Trio “Instrumental only, no vocals. Intimate jazz trio. Warm tenor saxophone playing slow, searching phrases. Electric bass. Brushed snare very quiet. The feeling of a small jazz club at 1am with few people left. Sophisticated and slightly sleepy. 72 BPM. Warm acoustic recording texture.”

Prompt 14 — Old Money Garden Party “Instrumental only, no vocals. Classical crossover. String quartet playing a gentle, refined piece. The feeling of an English country garden in summer — expensive and calm. Unhurried. 95 BPM. Clean classical recording with natural room sound.”

Prompt 15 — Mediterranean Afternoon “Instrumental only, no vocals. Balearic lounge, tropical house. Soft marimba melody. Light percussion. Warm bass. Distant waves as texture element. The feeling of being on a white boat somewhere between islands in the afternoon. Relaxed and bright. 100 BPM.”

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Niche 4 — Fantasy RPG Soundscapes: The Dedicated Community
Tabletop roleplaying gamers search for specific environments — not genres, but places. “Tavern sounds,” “dark forest ambience,” “dungeon atmosphere.” They use these tracks during 4–6 hour gaming sessions, producing exceptional watch time per view.
This community is highly engaged and actively shares content that matches their needs precisely. One well-crafted track can spread through Discord servers and Reddit communities without any promotion.

Prompt 16 — Ancient Elven Forest “Instrumental only, no vocals. Fantasy ambient. Magical forest at dawn. Distant solo wooden flute playing a simple, ancient-sounding melody. Sparkling high synth textures like sunlight through leaves. Real birdsong as texture element. The feeling of a forest that is thousands of years old. Peaceful and slightly otherworldly. 62 BPM.”

Prompt 17 — Dwarven Underground Forge “Instrumental only, no vocals. Industrial fantasy soundscape. Rhythmic heavy metallic clanging as the primary texture. Deep cavern reverb. Roaring fire. Low brass notes suggesting scale and power. The feeling of a forge deep underground. Heavy and purposeful. 75 BPM.”

Prompt 18 — Warm Tavern Fireplace “Instrumental only, no vocals. Medieval folk ambient. Acoustic lute playing a simple, cheerful repeated phrase. Crackling fireplace as continuous texture. Very distant muffled sounds of people talking — unintelligible, just warmth. The feeling of arriving somewhere safe after a long journey. 84 BPM, D major.”

Prompt 19 — Crystal Cave Depths “Instrumental only, no vocals. Dark fantasy ambient. Dripping water echoing in a vast cave. A pure, glassy sustained tone suggesting crystalline structures. Low mysterious drone. The feeling of being deep underground surrounded by something beautiful and dangerous. Eerie and still. 48 BPM.”

Prompt 20 — Peaceful Village Morning “Instrumental only, no vocals. Medieval pastoral folk. Solo acoustic lute. Gentle morning breeze as texture. The feeling of waking up in a small village in a fantasy world where nothing urgent is happening. Simple and warm. 78 BPM. Clean, intimate recording.”

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Niche 5 — Healing Frequencies and Meditation: The Highest CPM Audio Category on YouTube
This is the most profitable niche in AI-generated audio content.
The audience — wellness seekers, meditators, people using sound for sleep and anxiety — is large, consistent, and highly engaged. Advertisers in health, wellness, and mindfulness products pay premium CPM rates. A well-positioned video in this niche with strong retention can generate advertising revenue that compounds over years.

Prompt 21–528Hz Restoration Tone “Instrumental only, no vocals. Healing frequency ambient. 528Hz foundation tone as a clean, sustained sine wave. Angelic synth pad floating above it in harmony. The feeling of cellular restoration and deep peace. 45 BPM, C major. Sustained. Minimal variation. No percussion. Designed for extended listening.”

Prompt 22 — Grounding Frequency Meditation “Instrumental only, no vocals. Deep meditation ambient. Very low, slow bass frequency as foundation. Tibetan singing bowl tones entering and fading slowly. The feeling of being completely connected to the ground beneath you. Heavy and still. 38 BPM. No melody. Pure frequency texture.”

Prompt 23 — Beta Wave Focus Session “Instrumental only, no vocals. Focus enhancement ambient. Cle
